chapter_title: Raw hides and skins (other than furskins) and leather

inclusions.description: Raw hides and skins of bovine (including buffalo) or equine animals: fresh, salted, dried, limed, pickled or otherwise preserved — BUT NOT tanned, parchment-dressed or further prepared; whether or not dehaired or split

Raw skins of sheep or lambs: fresh, salted, dried, limed, pickled or otherwise preserved, but not tanned/parchment-dressed/further prepared; with or without wool on or split. Excludes premium furskins named in Note 1(c) (Astrakhan, Broadtail, Caracul, Persian/similar lambs, Indian/Chinese/Mongolian/Tibetan lambs)

Other raw hides and skins of other animals: fresh, salted, dried, limed, pickled or otherwise preserved, but not tanned/parchment-dressed/further prepared; dehaired or split. Excludes birdskins (Note 1(b)) and premium furskins (Note 1(c)). Includes: reptiles, swine, other

Tanned or crust hides and skins of bovine (including buffalo) or equine animals, WITHOUT hair on, whether or not split, but NOT FURTHER PREPARED. Wet state (wet-blue/chrome-tanned) and dry state (crust). Note 2(A): excludes reversibly tanned hides (those → back to 41.01)

Tanned or crust skins of sheep or lambs, without wool on, whether or not split, but not further prepared. Wet state (wet-blue) and dry state (crust). Note 2(B): 'crust' includes retanned, coloured or fat-liquored hides dried
Tanned or crust hides and skins of OTHER animals (goats/kids, swine, reptiles, other), without wool or hair on, whether or not split, but not further prepared. Wet state and dry state

Leather FURTHER PREPARED after tanning or crusting (including parchment-dressed leather) of bovine or equine animals, without hair on, split or not. Excludes chamois/patent/metallised leather of 41.14. Includes: full grains, grain splits, other — whole hides and sides
Leather further prepared after tanning/crusting of sheep or lamb, without wool on, split or not (other than 41.14)
Leather further prepared after tanning/crusting of OTHER animals (goats/kids, swine, reptiles, other), without wool or hair on (other than 41.14)
Special finish leathers: chamois leather (including combination chamois); patent leather and patent laminated leather; metallised leather

Composition leather (Note 3: the Nomenclature-wide definition of 'composition leather') with basis of leather or leather fibre, in slabs/sheets/strip/rolls. Also: parings and waste of leather or composition leather not suitable for making leather articles; leather dust/powder/flour

exclusions.condition: Parings or similar waste of raw hides or skins
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 05.11 (animal products NES, including raw hide waste) — Note 1(a)

exclusions.condition: Birdskins or parts of birdskins with feathers or down still attached
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 05.05 (feathers/down) or 67.01 (birdskin articles with feathers) — Note 1(b)

exclusions.condition: Premium furskins WITH hair/wool on — Chapter 43 animals: Astrakhan, Broadtail, Caracul, Persian lambs; Indian/Chinese/Mongolian/Tibetan lambs; Yemen/Mongolian/Tibetan goats & kids
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 43 (furskins and artificial fur) — Note 1(c). NOTE: Most common livestock hides WITH hair (bovine, equine, swine, chamois, regular sheep, goats, deer, camel) STAY in 41.01 or 41.03

exclusions.condition: Hides/skins with hair/wool that have been TANNED or DRESSED — if they are furskin species (Ch.43)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 43 — Note 1(c). But tanned bovine/equine/swine/chamois/deer hides with hair can stay in Ch.41 only if falling within the permitted species list of Note 1(c)

exclusions.condition: Reversibly tanned (pre-tanned) hides — tanning process is reversible
exclusions.action: Redirect back to headings 41.01–41.03 as appropriate — Note 2(A). Headings 41.04–41.06 require IRREVERSIBLE tanning. Reversibly tanned hides are treated as raw

exclusions.condition: Leather articles (footwear, handbags, saddlery, garments) — worked leather beyond slab/sheet/strip form
exclusions.action: Redirect to the appropriate article chapter: footwear → Ch.64; handbags → Ch.42; saddlery → Ch.42; leather garments → Ch.43 (leather clothing) or Ch.61/62

classification_rules.rule: Note 1(c) — Critical species distinction: Ch.41 vs Ch.43 for hides with hair/wool
classification_rules.description: This is the most complex boundary in Chapter 41. Hides/skins WITH hair/wool on: (A) MUST GO TO CHAPTER 43 if from premium fur species: Astrakhan/Broadtail/Caracul/Persian lambs; Indian/Chinese/Mongolian/Tibetan lambs; Yemen/Mongolian/Tibetan goats & kids. (B) STAY IN CHAPTER 41 if from: bovine (including buffalo), equine, swine (including peccary), chamois, gazelle, camel (including dromedary), reindeer, elk, deer, roebuck, dog — AND ordinary sheep/lambs and ordinary goats/kids not named above. The species and whether the skin is from a premium fur animal is the sole test.

The two-state tanning test: REVERSIBLE tanning (pre-tanning that can be undone) → hides remain in 41.01–41.03 (raw). IRREVERSIBLE tanning → moves to 41.04–41.06 (tanned/crust). This is a technical chemical process distinction. Wet-blue (chrome-tanned wet state) is irreversible and goes to 41.04–41.06.

The term 'crust' in headings 41.04–41.06 INCLUDES hides and skins that have been: retanned, coloured, or fat-liquored (stuffed/fatliquored) — PROVIDED they were dried before further preparation. This means crust leather is not just air-dried tanned hide; it includes those that received secondary chemical treatment before the final drying step.

Throughout the entire HS Nomenclature, 'composition leather' means ONLY the substances of heading 41.15 — i.e., bonded leather made from leather fibres/shavings compressed/bonded with binders into sheets/slabs. This is the legal definition that applies whenever 'composition leather' appears in any other heading's description.

classification_rules.rule: State-based classification within Chapter 41: raw → tanned/crust → further prepared
classification_rules.description: The chapter follows a clear processing chain: (1) RAW (41.01–41.03): fresh/preserved but not tanned; (2) TANNED/CRUST (41.04–41.06): irreversibly tanned but not further prepared — wet-blue or dry crust; (3) FURTHER PREPARED (41.07–41.14): leather that has received additional treatment after tanning (splitting, buffing, finishing, coating, patent finish, chamois, metallising). Special finish → 41.14 (chamois, patent, metallised). (4) COMPOSITION/WASTE (41.15).

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 41.04–41.06 vs 41.07/41.12/41.13 — 'not further prepared' threshold
classification_rules.description: 41.04–41.06 = tanned/crust leather 'not further prepared'. Any additional processing after tanning/crusting (buffing, finishing, lacquering, splitting into precise thicknesses for retail, embossing, etc.) → 41.07 (bovine/equine), 41.12 (sheep/lamb), or 41.13 (other animals). The 'further prepared' threshold distinguishes intermediate industrial material from finished leather.

classification_rules.rule: Wet-blue vs dry crust subheading split within 41.04–41.06
classification_rules.description: Within headings 41.04, 41.05 and 41.06, the subheadings split into: WET STATE (including wet-blue): chrome-tanned hides stored wet with preservatives. DRY STATE (crust): tanned hides that have been dried. This is purely a presentation/processing state distinction, not a species distinction.
